response:
Here's the complete pytest test suite covering all required cases, aligned with the behavior of your `is_valid_email` function:

```python
import pytest
# Import your function (adjust the import path to match your project structure)
from your_module import is_valid_email

# Test all valid emails (happy path + edge case valid inputs)
@pytest.mark.parametrize("valid_email", [
    # Happy path standard valid emails
    "test@example.com",
    "user.name+tag@domain.co.uk",
    "abc123@sub.domain.org",
    # Edge case valid inputs
    "x@y.io",  # Minimum length valid email
    "user_name%123@my-domain.com",  # All allowed special characters
    "TestUser99@EXAMPLE.COM",  # Uppercase letters are permitted
    "a_b.c%d+e-f@123.abc",  # All allowed special chars in local + numeric domain
])
def test_valid_emails(valid_email):
    assert is_valid_email(valid_email) is True

# Test invalid emails that return False (error and edge case invalid inputs)
@pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_email", [
    "",  # Empty string
    "testexample.com",  # Missing required @ symbol
    "user@name@example.com",  # Multiple @ symbols
    "test@example.c",  # TLD only 1 character (violates {2,} regex requirement)
    "test user@example.com",  # Space in local part
    "test!@example.com",  # Disallowed special character in local part
    "test@exa#mple.com",  # Disallowed special character in domain
    "test@localhost",  # No TLD/dot in domain
    "<test@example.com>",  # Extra invalid wrapper characters
    "test@.com",  # Empty domain segment before TLD
    "@example.com",  # Empty local part
    "test@example",  # No TLD at all
    "test+tag@example+.com",  # Disallowed + character in domain
    " test@example.com",  # Leading whitespace
    "test@example.com ",  # Trailing whitespace
])
def test_invalid_emails(invalid_email):
    assert is_valid_email(invalid_email) is False

# Test non-string inputs that raise type errors
@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_input", [
    None,
    12345,
    3.14,
    ["test@example.com"],
    {"email": "test@example.com"},
    True,
])
def test_non_string_input_raises_type_error(bad_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match="expected string or bytes-like object"):
        is_valid_email(bad_input)
```

### Test Coverage Breakdown:
1. **Happy Path**: Covers standard, real-world valid emails that meet all regex requirements
2. **Edge Cases**:
   - Minimum length valid email
   - All allowed special characters in both local and domain parts
   - Uppercase letters (permitted by the regex)
3. **Error Cases**:
   - Malformed emails missing required components (@, TLDs)
   - Disallowed special characters in local/domain parts
   - Extra whitespace/invalid wrapper characters
   - Non-string inputs that trigger TypeErrors (the function only accepts string inputs)
